title: v1.4 Protocol Specifications Released
summary: A short update on how a specification release helps the atlas move from concept to referenceable system behavior.

bodySections.title: Why it Matters
bodySections.body: Specification releases matter because they shrink the gap between ecosystem storytelling and implementation detail.

bodySections.title: What changes for readers
bodySections.body: More public specification detail makes it easier for builders and researchers to move from analogy to reference. That raises the quality of technical discussion across the ecosystem.

bodySections.title: What to do next
bodySections.body: Use updates like this as a signal to revisit infrastructure and MPC articles. As the reference layer gets sharper, the atlas should become more precise about what Arcium guarantees and how those guarantees are realized.
